title: to_string() on &str / Cow<str> allocates unnecessarily in watch.rs and elsewhere
description: watch.rs line 101: file_path.to_string_lossy().to_string() — to_string_lossy() returns Cow<str>; if the path is valid UTF-8 (almost always), this is a no-op borrow. .to_string() on a Cow<str> unconditionally allocates a new String even when the Cow is already Borrowed. The idiomatic replacement is .into_owned() which avoids the copy when already owned, or just passing the Cow directly to functions that accept AsRef<str>. Additionally, relay.rs uses .map(|h| h.to_string()) on host_str() results (lines 77-78, 151-152, 977-978 in server.rs) when the result is only needed as &str for subsequent calls. These are systematic gratuitous allocations.
close_reason: Accepted: ponytail comment. .to_string() calls feed into owned fields/collections.
